reviews.review_msg: I took this class in a summer 2 years ago... the lecture was super dry and the contents are mostly based on the textbook. He had in class quizzes which we watch a video and write down answers to his question. But he wont tell you its a quiz after the video...the MT was long and the research paper is too much for an entry course!

Attendance at lectures is absolutely crucial, yet they are super boring. The research paper is heavy for an introductory class (15-20 pages), and with the exam that is 80% of your mark. I would recommend trying this course with someone else.

Do not expect an easy course out of him. Hayden is one of those professors who is more in tuned with actual research than teaching. His lectures do not have flow and expects most of your knowledge to come from the textbook. The assignments are poorly described as his lectures are purely oral and no online help was available, except via e-mail.

His lectures are extremely helpful and relevant to the textbook. The films he showed to the class is also very clear on the topics and he makes sure we know what he expects for the exam (one MT). His assignments are not hard to do and the final assignment is so easy, it's a blast!

This course came recommended to me by two different people.  I have no idea why. Hayden appears objective because he is impersonable. In reality he is very subjective and biased, especially against women. It's a shame he's so well respected world-wide. I lost faith in humanity over this professor.

He's rather impersonal/unapproachable and teaching looks like a chore for him. The course may seem easy, but he expects a lot for the mt. You're expected to write down EVERYTHING in extreme detail to get a good mark. He wrote the damn textbook so if one little thing is missing, he'll know. Class average for the mt was extremely low.

Eccentric is a fitting word. I took a distance ed course with him. I did well on the assignments, paper, and final exam. Yet, for some reason, I got only a B. This summer, he was supposed to offer another distance ed course. However, for some reason, he decided to offered it as a lecture course instead this semester, throwing off my program...
hard

He has a massive ego. yes it's true, in the archaeological world he is considered amazing at what he does. His experience is phenomenal but don't bother going to him for help outside of class he'll trivialize what you do and what you ask, and his answers are always unclear because his attitude makes you not want to ask him anything else.

Seems like a knowledgeable guy. Teaching isn't really his thing though. He gives unclear instructions in regards to assignments and he difficult to talk to. He is your classic academic snob. Anything outside of lecture is definitely treated as if it?s a chore and a nuisance to him.

He seems like a perfectly fine, normal and approachable prof during lectures. However, one-on-one he is arrogant, rude, and generally unhelpful. It is my hope that SFU replaces him because this has been the only course at the University in which I had a particularly bad experience.

VERY BORING lectures.  he wasn't  helpful & hard to reach him, especially since he doesn't give out his e-mail.  Also, not very friendly (doesn't seem to like his students).  TA was really helpful, but need to attend his lecs b/c it's on his mt.
Great straightforward prof. Easy to learn from. Does seem a bit remote. I never had to approach him for help etc.Loved the subject and his new way of looking at archaeology.Recommend this class.

1.This course is not about ancient ppls/places. Prepare to memorize HIS accepted interpretations (the word "I" appears so much in the text it's ridiculous-yes he wrote the text) 2. He makes the study as boring as possible, and the text clearly rejects thinking upon ideas which are "already rejected" 3. TM totally useless, D.Evans

he has toooo high expectations. he answers unclearly when asking quesions. I was intereted in archaeology, but after taking this class with him i decided not to take any more arch courses. arch 100 felt definetly like a 300,400 course for me.

very egocentric and condescending. only his opinion is right. unapproachable and not helpful at all. is not clear, does not specify expectiations, or instructions for assignments. likes to embarrass students.

He seems very awkward. I went to ask him for help a couple of times, and each time, he would be awkward, and look at me as though I'm ridiculous. He is not friendly, and extremely abrupt outside of class. Definately not helpful. Take this course with another prof. He has too high expectations for a 100 level course for Beginners in archeology

One of the best classes I have taken. Interesting assignments. Took by dist ed. The tutor/marker was great--one of the most helpful I've ever had. I wish I could take another Arch course. If you work hard you will be rewarded with a decent grade.
I liked Brian. He's a good man, lots of people are all harshed out with this guy, but straight-up, he's a really informed, passionate, and interesting prof. take his class.

I took another class with Brian several semesters ago, and decided to take this because I enjoyed the last class so much. Needless to say, it was exactly what I expected. Always interesting, the topics were well organized, and Brian is incredibly well informed. Make sure you read the textbook thoroughly, but don't stress over small details.
pretty good, get ready to aggrandize
dramatic and unclear at times but I did not mind going to every class and staring at this handsome man! woof!

Very mysterious guy, explores interesting topics. I liked this class even though lec style is a little hard to follow. Loves showing old style films/reels. Pay attention to the fast and low toned remarks he makes in lecture: these are clues to what is important for the exam. No final!

Expectations are not clearly defined and instructions for bizarre assignments are incomplete but your mark is still penalized. He is clearly very smart, more of an academic than a teacher. I appreciated his insights. The course material is easy and its pretty easy to get a good mark on the midterm (worth 40%) by regurgitating it. No final exam!

Brian is a chill guy who brings in lots of props and visual aids- I liked this a lot. Answered all questions in class very well. Has good logic and reasoning, but sometimes you can get lost in what he is talking about.  His sentences are too long and a little indirect. He has a certain mystique about him. Clearly is a fresh thinker.

So far the best prof. I had. Lectures were a joy, lots of videos, artifacts, visuals. Great character, great scholar. I love him as a person and as a teacher. That's how a prof shoud be! Exams are fairly easy, marks are generous. Highly recommended.

The best prof I had so far. He knows his stuff, makes lectures v. interesting, lots of visual learning methods, very intelligent, charming personality. Ah... and he doesn't think he is the smartest person in the room. I took another course in arch. just because I like the man.
Ive taken 3 archaeology courses with Brian, didnt like him for the lower level class at all. But for the upper level stuff the guy is a champ prof. Encourages his class to think outside the box.
The *stupidest* class I have taken at sfu.  Absolutely useless and completely pointless!  As for Profesor Hayden, I found him to be very arrogant and unhelpful.
Awesome! makes you think in a new way. Very unique teaching style and fairly excentric. I loved his class!

Good class. Professor Hayden really knows his stuff and he makes the class very interesting. Lots of movies and he brings in real stone tools / weapons etc. His lectures can be a bit disorganized though.

It's been said Dr. Hayden's a "hard marker". That's an artifact of students not fully understanding the new way of thinking he advocates: actually think and don't regurgitate a textbook! Wonderful prof: really gets into his lectures and makes them interesting. I never wanted to skip class.
Very very good prof. Used a variety of media to teach, got us to do hands on work (making stone tools, etc.) Highly recommend!

Prof. Hayden is clearly a very intelligent man who knows his stuff. He gave great demos of prehistoric tool making (he actually made them!) and he really gets into his lectures. Generally, he was quite clear, but there was a few moments where he lost me. Assignments relevent and fair.
Man...he was hard
Interesting class, good teacher. Inspires interest in his topic.
Interesting course,lots of work, very hard grader. Okay but won't help your GPA.
No Comments
absoluty phenominal! thinks in a whole new way, demonstrates aboriginial weapons
